definition: Make (something bad or unsatisfactory) better.
definitionTranslation: 改善（糟糕或令人不满意的情况）。
example: The reform did much to ameliorate living standards.
exampleTranslation: 这项改革大大改善了生活水平。

definition: Deviating from what is standard, normal, or expected.
definitionTranslation: 偏离标准、正常或预期的。
example: An anomalous situation.
exampleTranslation: 一种反常的情况。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: an- (否定) + 词根: homalos (均匀)

definition: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.
definitionTranslation: 缺乏独创性，以至于显得平庸和乏味。
example: Songs with banal, repeated words.
exampleTranslation: 歌词平庸、重复的歌曲。

definition: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior.
definitionTranslation: 情绪或行为经常突然和无法解释地改变的。
example: A capricious and often brutal administration.
exampleTranslation: 一个反复无常且往往残酷的政府。

definition: Imitative of the work of another person.
definitionTranslation: 模仿他人作品的。
example: An artist who is not in the slightest bit derivative.
exampleTranslation: 一位毫不具有模仿痕迹的艺术家。

definition: Destroy completely; put an end to.
definitionTranslation: 彻底摧毁；结束。
example: This disease has been eradicated from the world.
exampleTranslation: 这种疾病已从世界上根除。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: e- (出) + 词根: radix (根)

definition: Not having any serious purpose or value.
definitionTranslation: 没有严重目的或价值的。
example: Rules to stop frivolous lawsuits.
exampleTranslation: 旨在阻止轻浮诉讼的规则。

definition: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
definitionTranslation: 喋喋不休，特别是谈论琐事。
example: Polonius is portrayed as a foolish, garrulous old man.
exampleTranslation: 波洛纽斯被描绘成一个愚蠢、喋喋不休的老人。

definition: Of, based on, or serving as a hypothesis.
definitionTranslation: 关于、基于或用作假设的。
example: That option is merely hypothetical at this juncture.
exampleTranslation: 在目前这个关头，那个选择仅仅是个假设。

definition: Making marks that cannot be removed.
definitionTranslation: 留下无法去除的印记的。
example: His story made an indelible impression on me.
exampleTranslation: 他的故事给我留下了难以磨灭的印象。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: in- (不) + 词根: delere (破坏)

definition: Place or deal with close together for contrasting effect.
definitionTranslation: 把...并列或并置以进行对比。
example: Black-and-white photos of slums were starkly juxtaposed with color images.
exampleTranslation: 贫民窟的黑白照片与彩色图片形成了鲜明对比。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: juxta (紧挨) + 词根: poser (放置)

definition: Relating to or resulting from motion.
definitionTranslation: 与运动相关的或由运动产生的。
example: Kinetic energy is the energy of motion.
exampleTranslation: 动能是运动产生的能量。

definition: Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic.
definitionTranslation: 受嗜睡症影响的；迟缓且冷漠的。
example: I felt tired and a little lethargic.
exampleTranslation: 我感觉累了，而且有点昏昏欲睡。

definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
definitionTranslation: 对细节非常关注的；十分小心且精确的。
example: He had always been so meticulous about his appearance.
exampleTranslation: 他总是对自己的外表一丝不苟。

definition: Wicked or criminal.
definitionTranslation: 邪恶的或犯罪的。
example: The nefarious activities of the organized-crime syndicates.
exampleTranslation: 有组织犯罪集团的邪恶活动。

definition: Render obscure, unclear, or unintelligible.
definitionTranslation: 使变得模糊、不清晰或难以理解。
example: The spelling changes will deform some familiar words and obfuscate their etymological origins.
exampleTranslation: 拼写的变化将使一些熟悉的单词变形，并使它们的词源来源变得模糊。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: ob- (遮掩) + 词根: fuscus (黑暗)

definition: Relieving pain without dealing with the cause of the condition.
definitionTranslation: 减轻疼痛而不处理病状原因的。
example: Orthodox medicines tend to be palliative rather than curative.
exampleTranslation: 传统医药往往是姑息性的而非根治性的。

definition: Extremely idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.
definitionTranslation: 极其理想主义的；不切实际的。
example: A vast and perhaps quixotic project.
exampleTranslation: 一个庞大而或许不切实际的项目。
morphologyTranslation: 词根: Don Quixote (堂吉诃德)

definition: Not or no longer needed or useful; superfluous.
definitionTranslation: 不再需要或不再有用的；多余的。
example: Many of the old skills had become redundant.
exampleTranslation: 许多老技能已经变得有些多余了。
morphologyTranslation: 前缀: re- (再) + 词根: undare (起伏)

definition: Make (something) by synthesis, especially chemically.
definitionTranslation: 特别是指通过化学方式合成（某物）。
example: Man synthesizes new chemical compounds.
exampleTranslation: 人类合成新的化合物。
